idea.hook: Imagine a moon holding TWICE Earth's entire ocean water!
facts: Earth's oceans contain approximately 1.3 billion cubic kilometers of water.
Jupiter's moon Europa is completely covered by an ice shell 15-25 km thick.
Beneath this ice, a global saltwater ocean is estimated to be around 100 km deep.
This hidden ocean contains roughly DOUBLE the liquid water of ALL Earth's oceans combined.
idea.payoff: A vast, dark, liquid water world potentially teeming with alien life, hidden beneath a frozen crust!
idea.title: Moon's Secret Ocean?! 🌊🤯

scenes.visual: A mysterious, icy moon (resembling Europa) slowly rotates against a dark, star-filled cosmic background. Focus is on its enigmatic presence.
Starts with a shot of Jupiter, then quickly zooms towards Europa. As Europa fills the screen, faint cracks become visible on its icy surface, hinting at what lies below.

scenes.text: ICE SHELL: 15-25 KM THICK / OCEAN DEPTH: ~100 KM
scenes.visual: A cross-section graphic of Europa slides in. First, a thick white/grey ice layer is highlighted with an arrow pointing to it, showing '15-25 km'. Then, the layer below reveals a vast blue ocean, with an arrow pointing to it showing '~100 km deep'.

A graphic comparison: A sphere representing Earth's total ocean volume (labeled 'Earth's Oceans') appears on one side. Then, a second, larger sphere (twice the size) representing Europa's hidden ocean appears next to it, labeled 'Europa's Ocean (2X)'.

scenes.text: A VAST, DARK WORLD. POTENTIAL FOR LIFE.
scenes.visual: An immersive shot of a deep, dark blue liquid ocean with faint bioluminescent specks or simple microbial shapes slowly drifting. Subtle glow from distant hydrothermal vents. Europa's distant silhouette might be visible in the background, implying its location.
